hero.title: AI-powered bioacoustics, at scale.
hero.subtitle: BirdNET uses deep learning to recognize over 6,000 species globally. Our open-source tools provide robust workflows for large-scale biodiversity monitoring and citizen science.
section.researchTools.title: The AI toolkit for conservation.
section.researchTools.subtitle: Open-source models and interfaces designed to scale from individual observations to continental monitoring programs.
section.researchTools.biogeoPriorsDesc: Refine species lists using precise coordinates and date metadata.
section.researchTools.cliDesc: Optimized headless analysis for massive acoustic deployments.
section.researchTools.embeddedDesc: Lightweight model versions for low-power edge computing or mobile devices.
section.researchTools.openSourceDesc: Contribute code to our core repositories or join our effort by sharing your data.
section.cards.ecologistsDesc: Process months of PAM data with high precision. Filter results by location and time to reduce false positives.
section.cards.developersDesc: Integration-ready Python modules and TFLite models. Access our codebase and community-driven tools.
section.cards.citizenScientistsDesc: Contribute to global biodiversity monitoring by using the BirdNET app to record and identify birds in your area.
section.cards.bioacousticResearchDesc: Scientific publications detailing the models, validation studies, and ecological impact of BirdNET.
section.architecture.title: How BirdNET works
section.architecture.subtitle: BirdNET processes raw acoustic data through a multi-stage pipeline designed for ecological accuracy. By transforming varying soundscapes into standardized feature representations, the system can isolate and identify subtle vocal signatures across thousands of species while accounting for biogeographical variations in species distribution.
workflow.step1.title: 1. Capture
workflow.step1.desc: Audio is captured at 48 kHz and divided into 3-second segments, optimizing the balance between model input size and the natural duration of avian vocalizations.
workflow.step2.title: 2. Spectrogram
workflow.step2.desc: Signals are processed into two log-scaled Mel-spectrograms, visualizing frequency patterns from 0 to 3 kHz and 150 Hz to 15 kHz for detailed analysis.
workflow.step3.title: 3. Neural Net
workflow.step3.desc: A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) scans these visuals, utilizing millions of trained weights to detect species-specific patterns.
workflow.step4.title: 4. Result
workflow.step4.desc: Initial predictions are cross-referenced with local metadata (location and date) to produce high-confidence species probabilities.
section.ecosystem.title: A complete stack for research.
section.ecosystem.subtitle: BirdNET is more than a model—it's an integrated ecosystem of tools designed to fit into your existing research workflow, from raw audio processing to statistical modeling in R.
section.ecosystem.pythonPackageDesc: Native Python integration for developers and data scientists. Build custom pipelines or run large-scale inference on your own infrastructure.
section.ecosystem.birdnetRDesc: The bridge to R-based ecological analysis. Clean, filter, and summarize BirdNET outputs directly within your R environment for occupancy modeling and trend analysis.
section.ecosystem.birdnetToolsDesc: User-friendly utilities for researchers. Access graphical interfaces for managing thousands of detections, verifying species, and preparing data for publication.
section.community.title: Built with BirdNET
section.community.subtitle: BirdNET powers a diverse ecosystem of external tools, from specialized field hardware to global data visualization platforms.
section.impact.title: A peer-reviewed foundation for bioacoustics.
section.impact.subtitle: BirdNET is built on a foundation of rigorous research. Our work focuses on developing reliable, open-source tools that transform raw audio into peer-reviewed ecological data.
section.impact.fireEcologyDesc: Our research in the Sierra Nevada mountain range evaluates how forest restoration and fire regimes affect bird occupancy, helping land managers understand biodiversity recovery in fire-adapted ecosystems.
section.impact.communityDynamicsDesc: We quantify how survey design, spatial coverage, and recording duration influence species richness estimates, providing a rigorous benchmark for monitoring shifts in avian community composition.
section.impact.researchOutreachDesc: We provide tools and documentation for K-12 programs and workshops, supporting the next generation of researchers in bioacoustics.
section.impact.publicationsArchiveDesc: Explore peer-reviewed papers and conference proceedings.
section.impact.technicalResourcesDesc: Access documentation, datasets, and research tools.
section.cta.title: Support the Future of Bioacoustics
section.cta.subtitle: BirdNET is an open-source research project powered by global collaboration. Whether you're a developer contributing code or a birder recording sounds, your help is vital.
footer.description: Advancing nature conservation through deep learning. A collaboration between the K. Lisa Yang Center for Conservation Bioacoustics and Chemnitz University of Technology.
about.label: THE COLLABORATION
about.title: About BirdNET
about.intro: BirdNET is a research collaboration using machine learning to monitor global biodiversity. We develop open-source tools that transform bioacoustics from a specialized field into a scalable solution for conservation.
about.mission.label: MISSION
about.mission.title: Science-led conservation.
about.mission.desc: BirdNET aims to lower the barrier to using sound for biodiversity monitoring. By combining deep learning with open tools and citizen science, we help track bird populations and support conservation decisions at local to global scales.
about.mission.precisionDesc: High-quality animal sound identification for researchers.
about.collab.label: COLLABORATION
about.collab.desc: BirdNET is a joint effort between:
about.collab.cornell: K. Lisa Yang Center for Conservation Bioacoustics, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
about.collab.tuc: Chair of Media Informatics, Chemnitz University of Technology
about.collab.supported: Supported by researchers, engineers, educators, and community contributors.
about.acoustic.title: Acoustic Monitoring
about.acoustic.desc: Many bird species are more easily detected by sound than sight. Passive monitoring captures activity without human disturbance across remote habitats and seasons.
about.ml.title: Machine Learning
about.ml.desc: Manual review of audio is not feasible. AI automates species identification at scale, extracting features from noisy soundscapes with consistent precision.
about.ecosystem.title: Open Ecosystem
about.ecosystem.desc: BirdNET provides core models for edge devices and reusable embeddings, supporting a global community of researchers and conservationists.
about.process.label: PROCESS & DATA
about.process.title: From soundscapes to insights
about.process.desc: The BirdNET model is trained on thousands of hours of curated bird vocalizations. It is optimized to be robust against background noise while remaining efficient enough for real-time mobile use.
about.process.trainingDesc: Curated audio recordings from large public collections like Xeno-canto and Macaulay Library segmented into 3-second windows, noise-filtered, and augmented for robustness.
about.process.architectureDesc: Deep convolutional neural networks with an EfficientNet backbone and custom spectrogram layer producing embeddings and per-species confidence scores based on visual patterns.
about.design.title: Design Priorities
analyzer.label: Research & Tools
analyzer.title: BirdNET-Analyzer
analyzer.intro: BirdNET-Analyzer is the workhorse tool for large-scale animal sound analysis, built for scientific workflows and long-term acoustic monitoring.
analyzer.overview.label: OVERVIEW
analyzer.overview.title: Built for scale.
analyzer.overview.desc: BirdNET-Analyzer applies state-of-the-art deep learning models to your audio recordings, transforming thousands of hours of sound into structured ecological data.
analyzer.batchDesc: Process entire directories of recordings automatically.
analyzer.confidenceDesc: Precise probability estimates for every detection.
analyzer.spatialDesc: Filter suggestions by location and date.
analyzer.multiFormatDesc: Support for MP3, WAV, FLAC, and more.
analyzer.customListsDesc: Restrict analysis to specific species of interest.
analyzer.ravenDesc: Direct export to Raven Pro and Audacity.
analyzer.features.label: FEATURES
analyzer.features.title: Advanced capabilities.
analyzer.features.throughputDesc: Process massive datasets using the CLI with multithreading and batch support. Host your own analysis API with the integrated server for distributed workflows.
analyzer.features.customTrainingDesc: Train custom classifiers on top of BirdNET embeddings to identify new species or sounds. Supports append/replace modes and automatic hyperparameter tuning.
analyzer.features.embeddingsDesc: Extract high-dimensional feature vectors (embeddings) to search for similar sounds, perform clustering, or identify acoustic patterns in unlabeled data.
analyzer.features.evaluationDesc: Assess model accuracy using ground-truth annotations. Compute detailed metrics including F1, Precision, Recall, AUROC, and Confusion Matrices.
analyzer.features.reviewDesc: Extract species-specific audio clips (segments) for manual validation. Use the Review Tab to systematically confirm detections and exported labeled data.
analyzer.features.developerDesc: Extensive command-line interface and Python module support for seamless integration into custom R or Python research pipelines.
analyzer.scientific.title: Scientific Workflow
analyzer.scientific.desc: BirdNET-Analyzer provides a complete end-to-end framework for bioacoustic research, from raw data to validated ecological insights.
analyzer.scientific.precisionDesc: Leverage multithreaded CLI analysis to process terabytes of data. Use the --batch_size argument to optimize for your CPU or GPU hardware.
analyzer.scientific.adaptiveDesc: Train classifiers using "Append" mode to add unique local species without losing the 6,000+ base species already in BirdNET.
analyzer.scientific.filteringDesc: Utilize the species range model (eBird-data powered) to automatically filter detections based on latitude, longitude, and week of the year.
analyzer.scientific.searchDesc: Generate sqlite-based scalar databases of 1024-dimensional feature vectors for rapid query-by-example sound searching.
analyzer.community.label: COMMUNITY & SUPPORT
analyzer.community.title: Get involved.
analyzer.community.issuesDesc: Found a bug or have a feature request? Our GitHub tracker is the best place to report issues. Please include your OS and Python version.
analyzer.community.codeDesc: We welcome pull requests for performance optimizations, new features, or documentation improvements. Check out our contribution guide.
analyzer.community.discussionsDesc: Have questions about analysis parameters or want to share your workflow? Join the conversation on GitHub Discussions.
analyzer.community.redditDesc: Connect with other BirdNET users, share your backyard monitoring setups, and troubleshoot with the community on r/BirdNET_Analyzer.
analyzer.security.title: Found a security issue?
analyzer.security.desc: Please do not open a public issue for security-related items. Email the maintainers directly via the contact information provided in the repository's README.
analyzer.help.title: Help us improve BirdNET-Analyzer
analyzer.help.desc: BirdNET-Analyzer is an open-source project. Your code contributions, bug reports, and data help us make it better for everyone.
app.label: Mobile Identification
app.title: BirdNET App
app.intro: Hold up your phone and identify birds with just one click. Fast, free, and powered by high-precision BirdNET AI—bringing professional bioacoustics to everyone.
app.stats.label: GLOBAL NETWORK
app.stats.title: Real-time Activity
app.stats.desc: BirdNET app users contribute to a global biodiversity database. Here is what's happening right now.
app.overview.label: OVERVIEW
app.overview.title: A research tool in your pocket.
app.overview.desc: The BirdNET mobile app lets you hold up your phone, record a few seconds of sound, and get instant identification suggestions. It uses the same core neural network models that power research-grade monitoring tools, simplified for everyday use.
app.oneClickDesc: Get immediate suggestions for birds as you hear them with a single tap.
app.confidenceScoresDesc: Understand the certainty of every identification.
app.globalCoverageDesc: Identify over 6,000 species across all continents.
app.citizenScienceDesc: Contribute your observations to global research.
app.workflow.label: WORKFLOW
app.workflow.title: Identification in four steps
app.workflow.recordDesc: Capture a high-quality audio snippet using your phone's built-in microphone.
app.workflow.analyzeDesc: The app generates a spectrogram and processes it through the BirdNET model.
app.workflow.suggestDesc: Receive a ranked list of species matches with associated confidence scores.
app.workflow.contributeDesc: Optionally share your observation to help scientists monitor biodiversity.
app.privacy.label: ETHICS & PRIVACY
app.privacy.title: Responsible citizen science.
app.privacy.desc: Your recordings help improve BirdNET. Submissions are used for research and are licensed under
app.privacy.speech: Human speech is filtered; we do not publish clips containing speech. Published examples are anonymized.
app.privacy.location: Location is optional and user‑controlled; when provided, it helps track species diversity and change over time.
app.privacy.secure: Data are stored securely to prevent unauthorized access.
app.privacy.research: Used for non‑commercial research and model development only.
app.privacy.commitmentDesc: We are dedicated to open science and the protection of bird populations. Every data point shared through the BirdNET app contributes to a deeper understanding of our planet's biodiversity.
app.tips.title: Technical Tips
app.tips.desc: For best results, move closer to the singing bird when possible and hold your device steady to minimize handling noise. Avoiding traffic or other background sounds will significantly improve the model's accuracy.
app.live.label: BROWSER ID
app.live.title: BirdNET Live
app.live.desc: Identify birds in real-time directly in your browser.
app.live.fullDesc: is a Progressive Web App (PWA) that processes sound locally on your device using TensorFlow.js—no audio ever leaves your phone.
app.support.label: SUPPORT
app.support.title: Feedback & assistance
app.support.desc: Encountering issues? Our team is active on GitHub and via email. Your feedback helps us improve the model and the application for everyone.
tools.label: BIOACOUSTIC STACK
tools.title: Open Source Tools
tools.intro: Open-source tools from the BirdNET-Team GitHub organization and collaborators. These projects bring BirdNET to research workflows, field devices, and analysis environments.
tools.repos.label: REPOSITORIES
demo.label: LIVE INFERENCE
demo.title: BirdNET Demo
demo.intro: Run BirdNET locally with TensorFlow.js. All audio is processed within your browser—nothing is uploaded.
demo.dropzone.drag: Drag & drop audio or choose a file (48 kHz preferred, WAV/MP3)
demo.recording.title: Recording...
demo.recording.info: Recording at 48 kHz for best results. Max 5 minutes.
demo.status.analysisComplete: Analysis complete. Click a species to see detections.
demo.status.audioLoaded: Audio loaded. Adjust settings and click Run Analysis.
demo.status.sampleLoaded: Sample loaded. Adjust settings and click Run Analysis.
demo.locationHelp: Adding your location helps filter species to those likely in your area.
showroom.label: COMMUNITY ECOSYSTEM
showroom.title: BirdNET Showroom
showroom.intro: BirdNET powers a growing ecosystem of community projects that bring bird sound recognition into homes, fieldwork, and creative tools.
showroom.yourProject.title: Your project here?
showroom.yourProject.desc: Working on a project that uses BirdNET models or embeddings? Get in touch and we may be able to feature it in this showroom.
showroom.haikubox.desc: A backyard listening station that continuously detects birds and shares short clips and species IDs via a web and mobile app, powered by BirdNET.
showroom.birdnetpi.desc: A Raspberry Pi-based station that runs BirdNET TFLite models on-device for real-time detection.
showroom.birdweather.desc: A real-time global map of stations detecting birds using BirdNET models.
showroom.birdnetlib.desc: A lightweight Python wrapper for BirdNET, enabling fast detections in scripts and applications.
showroom.ecopi.desc: A self-sufficient recording station for long-term bird sound monitoring using BirdNET-based models.
showroom.terra.desc: The Terra Station is a listening and radio monitoring device that streams nature sounds and identifies bird calls using BirdNET.
showroom.dawnchorus.desc: A global participation project inviting people to record dawn choruses with BirdNET-powered insights.
showroom.chirpity.desc: A fast desktop validation tool for BirdNET detections with waveform + spectrogram display.
showroom.birdnetgo.desc: A Golang implementation of BirdNET for fast, portable deployments.
showroom.birda.desc: A fast, cross-platform CLI tool for bird species detection using BirdNET and Google Perch AI models in Rust.
showroom.whobird.desc: An offline Android app built on BirdNET TFLite models for fully local bird sound ID.
showroom.ribbit.desc: A frog sound identification tool built on BirdNET embeddings.
showroom.ecosoundweb.desc: A web-based ecoacoustics analysis suite that integrates BirdNET.
showroom.faunanet.desc: A bioacoustics platform built on BirdNET-Analyzer and birdnetlib, providing a flexible environment for research projects and large-scale soundscape analyses.
showroom.muuttolintujen.desc: A mobile app developed at the University of Jyväskylä that lets citizens record bird songs and contribute observations using a BirdNET-based classifier tuned for Finnish birds.
showroom.birdomatic.desc: A web-based workbench for exploring millions of BirdNET-Analyzer detections, with rich filtering, plots, weather context, and access to spectrograms and audio files.
publications.label: SCIENTIFIC IMPACT
publications.title: Publications
publications.intro: Peer-reviewed research and conference proceedings that leverage BirdNET for large-scale ecological monitoring, machine learning development, and community-driven data collection.
resources.label: LEARNING CENTER
resources.title: Resources
resources.intro: A collection of tutorials, technical documentation, and community-driven guides to help you implement BirdNET in your research or personal projects.
resources.tutorials.label: TUTORIALS & WEBINARS
resources.tutorials.title: Video Guides
resources.video1.desc: An overview of how BirdNET's AI tools are used to automate large-scale audio analysis for bird identification, supporting conservation research and ecological monitoring.
resources.video2.desc: A presentation outlining the BirdNET project's potential for bird conservation, describing how the app and associated technologies support both broad monitoring and detailed research.
resources.video3.desc: This recorded webinar explores how solar-powered, real-time recording units—equipped with cutting-edge AI tools—can help land stewards detect species, monitor ecosystems, and share the sounds of their landscapes with the public.
resources.video4.desc: A practical video covering tips, tricks, and insights for using BirdNET and similar machine learning tools for ecoacoustic analysis, model interpretation, and workflow setup.
resources.video5.desc: A BioacousTalks session discussing extensions and applications of BirdNET-style acoustic classification methods to bat bioacoustics and broader acoustic detection contexts.
resources.video6.desc: A BioacousTalks presentation with Stefan Kahl (BirdNET creator) on building custom machine learning models using BirdNET Analyzer, useful for extending BirdNET to new taxa or environments.
resources.video7.desc: A video on using BirdNET models in real acoustic monitoring projects, showcasing community-level bioacoustic surveys and minimal local training adaptations.
resources.video8.desc: An introduction to using sound and machine learning for bird identification, reviewing how models like BirdNET classify audio signals from spectrograms.
k12.label: Education
k12.title: K‑12 Resources
k12.intro: Schlaumeise (SmartFinch) is an interdisciplinary educational and research project that bridges the gap between modern AI technology and nature observation for students of all ages.
k12.mission.label: MISSION
k12.mission.title: Bridging Nature and Technology
k12.mission.desc: Schlaumeise connects biology, computer science, and acoustics to inspire students (Grades 1–12) to explore the world of local birds while learning the foundations of Artificial Intelligence.
k12.identifyDesc: Discover which birds share your backyard or schoolyard.
k12.citizenScienceDesc: Contribute real data to the global BirdNET database.
k12.curriculum.label: CURRICULUM
k12.curriculum.title: Interdisciplinary Pillars
k12.curriculum.biologyDesc: Learn about birds as bioindicators and the importance of habitat protection.
k12.curriculum.acousticsDesc: Discover how sound works and how to read visual spectrograms of bird songs.
k12.curriculum.csDesc: Understand the basics of neural networks and how they learn to recognize sounds.
k12.curriculum.techDesc: Build your own recording stations using Raspberry Pi and other DIY components.
k12.phd.desc: As a PhD research project at TU Chemnitz (Media Informatics), Schlaumeise turns scientific research into exciting classroom experiences. Students learn to use technology not just as a tool, but as a window into the ecosystem.
k12.schlaumeise.title: Schlaumeise
k12.schlaumeise.desc: Interaktive Lernplattform mit Projekten rund um heimische Vögel, Akustik und KI — für Schule und Zuhause.
k12.smartfinch.title: SmartFinch
k12.smartfinch.desc: Kid‑friendly activities to explore birds, sound, and AI — from listening games to simple field devices.
k12.pedagogy.label: PEDAGOGICAL APPROACH
k12.pedagogy.title: Research Questions for Students
k12.pedagogy.q1: What makes a Blue Jay's call different from a Robin's song?
k12.pedagogy.q2: How does local noise pollution affect bird activity?
k12.pedagogy.q3: Can we predict bird migration based on local sound recordings?
k12.pedagogy.materials: We provide ready-to-use materials for educators, including assessment templates and project guides.
k12.maker.label: MAKER SPACE
k12.maker.title: DIY Recording Stations
k12.maker.desc: One of our most popular projects involves building a custom "Smart Finch" birdhouse. Using a Raspberry Pi and a small microphone, students create a persistent monitoring station that identifies visitors in real-time.
projectsMap.label: GLOBAL NETWORK
projectsMap.title: Projects Map
projectsMap.intro: Explore how BirdNET is being used by researchers around the world. Select a project to locate it on the map.
projectsMap.privacy.title: Map Privacy Notice
projectsMap.privacy.desc: To display the interactive project map, we load map tiles from external providers (OpenStreetMap and CARTO). These providers may process your IP address and other browser data.
projectsMap.privacy.learn: Learn more in our Privacy Policy
projectsMap.add.title: How to add your project
projectsMap.add.desc: We want to highlight the many use cases and great projects in bioacoustics and conservation that use BirdNET in their workflow. We need your help to add more projects to the map and keep the information accurate.
projectsMap.add.githubDesc: Submit a PR with a new entry in the
projectsMap.add.requiredNote: * Required fields for a valid map marker.
projectsMap.accurate.title: Keep it accurate
projectsMap.accurate.desc: If you would like to update a project or find that some information is incorrect, please let us know through any of the contribution channels.
tools.moreOnGithubDesc: This page highlights key repositories from the BirdNET-Team organization. For the full, up-to-date list of public projects, visit GitHub directly.
tools.collaborators: We are fortunate to collaborate with open-source contributors who develop tools that complement BirdNET. For more projects from our collaborators, check out the DBD Research Group and Perch-Team at Google Research on GitHub.
tools.built.title: Built something with BirdNET?
tools.built.desc: We love seeing how the community uses BirdNET. If you've developed a tool, library, or integration, let us know so we can feature it in our showroom.
about.funding.label: FUNDING & PARTNERS
about.funding.title: Supported by a global network
about.funding.institutionalDesc: Work at the K. Lisa Yang Center for Conservation Bioacoustics is made possible by the generosity of K. Lisa Yang, supporting innovative conservation technologies that scale global biodiversity monitoring.
about.funding.projectDesc: Development of BirdNET is supported by:
about.funding.researchDesc: BirdNET is a joint effort of partners from academia and industry. This multidisciplinary collaboration enables us to bridge the gap between AI research and practical field ecology.
about.workflow.label: RECOGNITION WORKFLOW
about.contact.title: Contact
about.contact.desc: Questions about BirdNET research, tools, or collaborations:
donate.impact.item1: Develop and train new BirdNET models for more species and regions.
donate.impact.item2: Maintain and scale infrastructure for apps, web tools, and APIs.
donate.impact.item3: Create open-source software for conservation and research workflows.
donate.impact.item4: Work with partners to apply BirdNET in real-world conservation projects.
donate.impact.closing: Every donation, large or small, helps cover server costs and supports ongoing research and development.
contribute.label: GET INVOLVED
contribute.title: Contribute to BirdNET
contribute.intro: Advancing conservation through acoustic monitoring requires a global effort. Whether you are a developer, a data scientist, or a bird enthusiast, there are many ways to help.
contribute.code.title: Code & Open Source
contribute.code.desc: BirdNET is built on open-source principles. We maintain several repositories on GitHub, ranging from our core analysis engine to mobile and web implementations.
contribute.code.help: Help us improve our tools by:
contribute.code.list1: Contributing to the BirdNET-Analyzer codebase.
contribute.code.list2: Developing new integrations for R, Python, or embedded systems.
contribute.code.list3: Reporting bugs and suggesting new features on our issue trackers.
contribute.code.list4: Improving documentation to make our tools more accessible.
contribute.data.title: Data & Expertise
contribute.data.desc: High-quality, annotated audio data is the backbone of BirdNET. Your recordings and expertise can directly improve the accuracy of our models.
contribute.data.list1: Upload to Xeno-canto: Share your bird recordings on xeno-canto.org. We regularly use these recordings for model training.
contribute.data.list2: Annotated Soundscapes: If you have fully annotated soundscapes (long-form recordings with species labels and timestamps), please let us know.
contribute.data.list3: Expert Validation: Help us validate detections in your region to ensure our models reflect local biodiversity accurately.
contribute.data.contact: Every recording helps us advance conservation bioacoustics. Feel free to reach out to us if you have datasets or expertise to share.
contribute.research.title: Research & Conservation
contribute.research.desc: We are always looking for new ways to apply BirdNET in the field. We collaborate with researchers and conservation organizations worldwide to turn acoustic data into actionable insights.
contribute.research.monitoringDesc: If you have a monitoring project that could benefit from AI expertise, we'd love to hear from you.
contribute.research.fundingDesc: We are open to joint funding proposals that advance bioacoustics and conservation technology.
contribute.research.contact: Interested in collaborating? Contact us to discuss your project.
contribute.students.title: Student Projects
contribute.students.desc: Are you a student looking for a thesis topic? We offer projects at Chemnitz University of Technology, often in collaboration with other institutions.
contribute.students.tinyml: TinyML: Deploying lightweight models on embedded devices and field sensors.
contribute.students.advancedId: Advanced ID: Individual bird identification and bat sound identification.
contribute.students.unsupervised: Unsupervised Learning: Species discovery and acoustic pattern recognition.
contribute.students.foundation: Foundation Models: Exploring large-scale pre-training for acoustic monitoring.
contribute.students.visibility: Our student projects offer immediate visibility through open-source development and the chance to contribute to a project used by thousands.
contribute.students.contact: Interested in a thesis project? Reach out to us with your background.
contribute.other.title: Other ways to help
contribute.other.desc: If you're not a developer or data specialist, you can still support the project by donating to help cover infrastructure and research costs.
donate.label: GIVING
donate.title: Support BirdNET
donate.intro: BirdNET is a research project that relies on external funding to improve models, build new tools, and keep services available to the global community.
donate.impact.label: IMPACT
donate.impact.title: Why your support matters
donate.contact.title: Contact the Lab
donate.contact.desc: If you have questions about supporting BirdNET or the K. Lisa Yang Center for Conservation Bioacoustics:
